perturbation.original_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Information to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Data to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" was changed to "Confidential Data". The rest of the section refers to "Confidential Information", this creates an in-text contradiction and it's now ambigious on wether Parties can disclose confidential information to a third party.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Data to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the first sentence of section 5. However, the rest of section 5, as shown in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, still refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because it's unclear whether the restriction on disclosing information to third parties applies to 'Confidential Information', 'Confidential Data', or both. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because the contract uses multiple terms that now have undefined boundaries.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Permitted Limited Disclosure of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, a Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent but only to the extent: (a) approved by the Disclosing Party in writing; or (b) required by Law or an Authority, but only if: (i) the Party attempts to notify the Disclosing Party as far in advance as practicable prior to making disclosure of its intent to disclose Confidential Information and of the content and mode of communication of the disclosure; and (ii) the Party cooperates with the Disclosing Party's efforts to obtain a protective order protecting the Confidential Information from disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Permitted Limited Disclosure of Sensitive Data. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, a Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent but only to the extent: (a) approved by the Disclosing Party in writing; or (b) required by Law or an Authority, but only if: (i) the Party attempts to notify the Disclosing Party as far in advance as practicable prior to making disclosure of its intent to disclose Confidential Information and of the content and mode of communication of the disclosure; and (ii) the Party cooperates with the Disclosing Party's efforts to obtain a protective order protecting the Confidential Information from disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" was changed to "Sensitive Data" at the beginning of the paragraph. But the paragraph continues to refer to "Confidential Information", this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 7. Permitted Limited Disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 7. Permitted Limited Disclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Permitted Limited Disclosure of Sensitive Data. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, a Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent but only to the extent: (a) approved by the Disclosing Party in writing; or (b) required by Law or an Authority, but only if: (i) the Party attempts to notify the Disclosing Party as far in advance as practicable prior to making disclosure of its intent to disclose Confidential Information and of the content and mode of communication of the disclosure; and (ii) the Party cooperates with the Disclosing Party's efforts to obtain a protective order protecting the Confidential Information from disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data' at the beginning of section 7, but the rest of the paragraph still refers to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text is in the same location and exhibits the exact same contradiction, creating an in-text contradiction because the paragraph now uses two different terms ('Sensitive Data' and 'Confidential Information') seemingly interchangeably within the same context, which causes confusion and uncertainty regarding the scope and applicability of the disclosure provisions. Therefore, it aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Confidential Information shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Confidential Data shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" has been changed to "Confidential Data". The rest of the section refers to "Confidential Information", this creates an in-text contradiction and introduces ambiguity regarding which specific information must be returned upon request.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Confidential Data shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "Confidential Information" to "Confidential Data" in the first sentence while the rest of the paragraph, as well as the contradicted text, continues to use "Confidential Information." This creates a Misaligned Terminology, as the contract now uses two different terms ('Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data') seemingly interchangeably without defining them, leading to uncertainty about which information is subject to the return obligation, which fits the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 11 . Disclaimer of Warranties. Each Party hereby disclaims and does not make any express or implied representation or warranty concerning the accuracy or completeness of Confidential Information and no Disclosing Party shall have liability to the Party for the Party’s use of Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 11 . Disclaimer of Warranties. Each Party hereby disclaims and does not make any express or implied representation or warranty concerning the accuracy or completeness of Protected Data and no Disclosing Party shall have liability to the Party for the Party’s use of Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential Information" was changed to "Protected Data" in the first part of the paragraph. The paragraph continues referencing the the "Confidential Information". This creates an in-text contradiction, since its no longer clear which kind of data is being disclaimed
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 11. Disclaimer of Warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions, 11. Disclaimer of Warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11 . Disclaimer of Warranties. Each Party hereby disclaims and does not make any express or implied representation or warranty concerning the accuracy or completeness of Protected Data and no Disclosing Party shall have liability to the Party for the Party’s use of Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Protected Data' in the first part of the paragraph but retains 'Confidential Information' later in the same paragraph (which is also the contradicted text). This creates an in-text contradiction because the paragraph now refers to two potentially distinct categories of information without clarifying which is meant to be covered by the disclaimer of warranties, making it unclear and internally inconsistent, as per the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition.
